name: Yum Pra-Hmik (Thai Squid "Salad").
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean and wash the fresh squids, removing all inner matter and skin. Cut through the side so that the meat is in a single "sheet". Cut the meat into strips about 3/4 inch to 1 inch wide and 2 inches long. If desired, slash one side in a close criss-cross pattern; the strip will curl into a roll upon cooking.
2. Place the squid pieces into a wire strainer with a long handle. Boil a pot of water and immerse the strainer with the squid meat into the boiling water to cook. For very fresh squid, cook until the meat has turned opaque and heated through. For frozen or "not so fresh" squid, it is advisable to cook a little longer.
3. Lift the squid out of the boiling water and let drain in the strainer.
4. Place the well-drained squid meat into a bowl and add fish sauce and lime or lemon juice. Adjust taste by adding more of either ingredient. The flavors should be tangy, sour with sufficient salty tastes.
5. Add lemongrass, ginger root (finely julienne), and chopped onions; mix well.
6. Place on a serving platter lined with lettuce leaves and topped with chopped scallions, cilantro, mint leaves, and crushed hot chili peppers.
